description: Implement Vela's full local-first layered retrieval architecture as a Ralph workflow, including repo, contract, workspace, and memory graph layers, identity and evidence plumbing, retrieval orchestration, and local developer validation through CLI, TUI, doctor, installation checks, setup wizard, and workflow automation.

userStories.title: Establish layered architecture model and shared types
userStories.description: As a developer, I need explicit shared types and boundaries for repo, contract, workspace, and memory layers so that the rest of the system can evolve without mixing responsibilities.
acceptanceCriteria: Define or update shared architecture-facing types under existing areas such as `pkg/types`, `internal/graph`, and `internal/query` for layer identity, evidence, and routing metadata
Represent the topology `organization -> workspace -> repo -> subsystem -> node/chunk` and keep memory entities separate from repo code entities
Document the layer responsibilities and boundaries in repo docs so they match `VELA_ARCHITECTURE.md`
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Start by stabilizing contracts before touching routing or fusion logic.

userStories.title: Solidify repo-local indexing and retrieval baseline
userStories.description: As a local developer, I need repo-local SQLite, FTS, and vector retrieval to be the correctness path so that Vela works predictably on one machine before any acceleration.
acceptanceCriteria: Update existing retrieval and storage areas such as `internal/retrieval/sqlite.go`, `internal/retrieval/vector.go`, and related tests to standardize repo-local metadata, lexical index behavior, and vector storage contracts
Keep embeddings stored locally with SQLite as the baseline execution path
Ensure retrieval remains repo-scoped and explainable rather than using a centralized deep index
Add or update tests covering repo-local indexing and query behavior
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Fallback correctness matters more than accelerator cleverness.

userStories.title: Unify extraction pipeline across supported source types
userStories.description: As a developer, I need extraction for code, docs, PDFs, and project-level inputs to feed the architecture consistently so that indexing layers share a stable ingestion contract.
acceptanceCriteria: Use existing extraction areas such as `internal/extract/code.go`, `internal/extract/doc.go`, `internal/extract/pdf.go`, `internal/extract/project.go`, and parser helpers as the primary integration surface
Ensure extracted outputs carry enough metadata to support repo graph insertion, chunking, retrieval, and evidence attribution
Support the current language and document mix already present in the repo without regressing extraction coverage
Add or update tests in `internal/extract/*_test.go` for representative code and document ingestion flows
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Do not let extractors invent graph-level identity locally.

userStories.title: Add contract graph ingestion and declared service evidence
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a contract-focused graph layer so that cross-repo service and API relationships come from declared artifacts instead of being guessed from code alone.
acceptanceCriteria: Introduce contract-layer ingestion and storage using existing graph and extraction boundaries under `internal/graph` and `internal/extract`
Represent declared service and interface entities separately from repo-local code nodes
Ensure workspace-level service bindings can consume contract evidence without overwriting declared truth with weaker derived signals
Add tests for declared artifact ingestion and contract-to-workspace linkage behavior
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Declared edges outrank derived guesses. Keep that invariant visible in tests.

userStories.title: Materialize workspace graph and routing metadata
userStories.description: As a user asking cross-repo questions, I need a lightweight workspace graph so that Vela can route to the right repositories before deep retrieval begins.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement workspace-level entities and edges using existing graph, query, and retrieval areas such as `internal/graph`, `internal/query`, and `internal/retrieval`
Support routing metadata for repos, packages, services, domains, and dependencies without turning the workspace layer into a deep code graph
Keep workspace graph derivation lightweight and compatible with repo refreshes
Add tests covering repo selection or routing from workspace context
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Workspace is routing truth, not code truth.

userStories.title: Materialize memory graph from Ancora-backed observations
userStories.description: As a developer, I need memory to be a first-class graph layer sourced from Ancora so that historical decisions, bugs, and preferences participate in retrieval with structure and provenance.
acceptanceCriteria: Build on existing memory-related areas such as `internal/ancora`, `internal/listener`, and `internal/extract/ancora.go` to ingest or sync observation data into a structured memory layer
Store observations and references separately from repo graph structure
Support links from memory entities to repos, files, symbols, concepts, workspaces, or organizations by reference
Add or update tests for Ancora ingestion or graph materialization behavior
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Memory is historical truth, not a dump of code symbols.

userStories.title: Implement identity resolver and evidence model
userStories.description: As a developer, I need canonical identity and typed evidence across layers so that joins, ranking, and explainability are consistent instead of ad hoc.
acceptanceCriteria: Add canonical key resolution and evidence metadata through shared areas such as `pkg/types`, `internal/graph`, and `internal/query`
Support evidence fields such as evidence type, source artifact, confidence, and verification state on graph relationships where relevant
Ensure cross-graph joins do not depend on each graph inventing identity locally
Add tests covering canonical resolution and evidence-aware ranking or merge behavior
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: If identity is sloppy, every later layer becomes untrustworthy.

userStories.title: Implement memory reference binder and stale reference handling
userStories.description: As a developer, I need memory references to stay live as code moves so that historical observations remain useful after refactors instead of silently rotting.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement binding or rebinding behavior between memory references and current code entities using memory and graph areas such as `internal/ancora`, `internal/graph`, and retrieval/query helpers
Represent reference states such as current, redirected, stale, and ambiguous with supporting evidence
Support safe rebind suggestions rather than silent mutation when confidence is uncertain
Add tests covering changed-code or redirected-reference scenarios
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Binder state must be queryable. Silent decay is unacceptable.

userStories.title: Build retrieval orchestrator across memory, workspace, contract, and repo layers
userStories.description: As a user, I need Vela to route first and retrieve deeply second so that answers use the right repos, the right historical context, and clear provenance.
acceptanceCriteria: Extend retrieval and query surfaces such as `internal/query/query.go`, `internal/query/search.go`, `internal/retrieval/traversal.go`, and graph helpers to classify, route, and fuse results
Support the architecture rule that workspace selects likely repos before repo-deep retrieval runs
Support memory-aware retrieval without treating memory as a substitute for repo-local code evidence
Return fused results with enough provenance to explain which layer contributed what
Add tests using architecture-shaped routing scenarios
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Favor route-first behavior over giant undifferentiated search.

userStories.title: Expose architecture behavior through CLI commands and explainable outputs
userStories.description: As a solo local developer, I need the CLI to expose extraction, search, path, explain, and query behavior over the new architecture so that the system is testable without hidden internals.
acceptanceCriteria: Update `cmd/vela/main.go` and related command files so the current CLI surface works with the layered retrieval model
Ensure commands such as extract, search, path, explain, query, and serve can operate against the architecture without breaking current expectations
Expose explainable output that distinguishes memory, workspace, contract, and repo evidence when relevant
Add or update command tests under `cmd/vela/*_test.go` and `internal/query/*_test.go` where appropriate
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: If the architecture only exists in internals, the product is not usable.

userStories.title: Validate local setup with doctor, installation checks, and Ancora-like wizard flow
userStories.description: As a local developer, I need a trustworthy setup and validation path so that I can install, configure, and verify Vela end-to-end before relying on retrieval results.
acceptanceCriteria: Use existing setup and validation anchors such as `internal/setup/wizard.go`, `internal/setup/*.go`, `internal/doctor/integration.go`, `internal/tui/doctor.go`, and related command wiring in `cmd/vela`
Doctor checks validate configured sources, runtime connectivity, graph health, and relevant local integrations instead of only checking config presence
Wizard flow validates system requirements, provider setup, installation readiness, and final verification in a way comparable to Ancora's setup guidance
Add or update tests for doctor and wizard flows in doctor, setup, and TUI test areas
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: A wizard that writes config but cannot verify readiness is fake confidence.

userStories.title: Deliver Ralph workflow automation, validation scenarios, and docs
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need a runnable Ralph workflow package and architecture validation docs so that this feature can be executed, resumed, and verified consistently.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `workflows/ralph/ralph.sh` in the Vela repo to run or manage the Ralph workflow for this feature
Keep `workflows/ralph/prd.json` aligned with the implemented story ordering and branch name
Document the workflow, setup expectations, and validation approach in repo docs using architecture examples as acceptance or benchmark scenarios
Include validation coverage for CLI, TUI, doctor, install flow, indexing, retrieval, and graph-layer behavior
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: The workflow itself is part of the product discipline.
